description: Wire the AI brain end-to-end: DB triggers → orchestrator → event chains → intelligence inbox + Slack DM. Make abilities transparent, self-healing, and progressively autonomous.

userStories.title: Ensure agent_trigger_runs table + fix rate limiting
userStories.description: As a platform engineer, I want the agent_trigger_runs table to exist and rate limiting to work correctly, so that trigger execution is observable and bounded.
acceptanceCriteria: Migration creates agent_trigger_runs table if not exists with all required columns
Index on (organization_id, created_at) for rate limit queries
Rate limit check handles missing table gracefully (returns error, not silent bypass)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Foundation — must exist before any triggers fire

userStories.title: DB triggers for deal events → agent-trigger
userStories.description: As a sales rep, I want deal creation and stage changes to automatically trigger agent workflows, so that my pipeline stays active without manual intervention.
acceptanceCriteria: PG trigger on deals INSERT calls agent-trigger via pg_net with event deal_created
PG trigger on deals UPDATE (stage column) calls agent-trigger with event deal_stage_changed
Trigger payload includes deal_id, organization_id, owner_id, stage (old+new), value
Follows ops_rule_event_triggers pattern (debounce, Vault auth)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: DB triggers for calendar + meeting events → agent-trigger
userStories.description: As a sales rep, I want new calendar events and completed meetings to automatically trigger prep and follow-up workflows.
acceptanceCriteria: PG trigger on calendar_events INSERT (external attendees) calls agent-trigger with calendar_event_created
PG trigger on meetings UPDATE (status=completed or recording_url set) calls agent-trigger with meeting_completed
Calendar payload includes event_id, user_id, org_id, start_time, attendees, title
Meeting payload includes meeting_id, owner_user_id, org_id, deal_id, recording_url
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: DB triggers for contact + task events → agent-trigger
userStories.description: As a sales rep, I want new contacts to be auto-enriched and overdue tasks to trigger escalation without checking manually.
acceptanceCriteria: PG trigger on contacts INSERT calls agent-trigger with contact_created
PG trigger on tasks UPDATE (due_date < now AND completed=false) calls agent-trigger with task_overdue
Task trigger has debounce (max once per task per 24h)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Fleet handoff routes for pre-call chain
userStories.description: As a platform engineer, I want the pre-call event chain defined in fleet tables so calendar events cascade through research → dossier → notification.
acceptanceCriteria: fleet_event_route: calendar_event_created → pre-meeting-prep sequence
fleet_sequence_definition: lead_research → company_analysis → pre_meeting_dossier → talking_points → notify_user
fleet_handoff_routes for each step completion → next step
Context mapping passes contact_id, company_id, meeting_id, start_time through chain
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Fleet handoff routes for post-call chain
userStories.description: As a platform engineer, I want the post-call event chain defined so completed meetings cascade through transcript → actions → follow-up → CRM update.
acceptanceCriteria: fleet_event_route: meeting_completed → post-meeting-followup-pack sequence
fleet_sequence_definition: transcript_extraction → action_items → followup_email_draft → crm_update → deal_create_or_update → cc_items
fleet_handoff_routes for each step completion → next step
Context mapping passes meeting_id, recording_url, deal_id, contact_ids through chain
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Pre-call: calendar event → lead research
userStories.description: As a sales rep, I want lead and company research to start automatically when a new meeting with an external attendee appears on my calendar.
acceptanceCriteria: agent-trigger handler for calendar_event_created identifies external attendees
Triggers lead-research or company-research skill via orchestrator
Stores results linked to calendar_event_id and contact_id
Fires handoff event lead_research.complete
Skips if research exists for contact within last 30 days
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Pre-call: research complete → pre-meeting dossier
userStories.description: As a sales rep, I want a meeting dossier auto-generated from research so I walk into calls prepared.
acceptanceCriteria: Handoff from lead_research.complete triggers pre-meeting-dossier skill
Dossier includes company summary, attendee profiles, deal context, talking points, potential objections
Stored as command_centre_item with item_type: meeting_prep
Fires handoff event pre_meeting_dossier.complete
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Pre-call: dossier ready → CC item + Slack DM
userStories.description: As a sales rep, I want to be notified when my meeting prep is ready via CC inbox and Slack DM at a configurable time before the call.
acceptanceCriteria: Creates command_centre_item with status ready, priority based on meeting imminence
Sends Slack DM with Block Kit: meeting title, time, attendees, talking points, View full brief button
Default timing: 30 minutes before meeting start (configurable per-ability)
Respects quiet hours and Slack rate limits
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Post-call: meeting completed → transcript + action items
userStories.description: As a sales rep, I want meeting transcripts processed and action items extracted automatically after every call.
acceptanceCriteria: agent-trigger handler for meeting_completed triggers transcript extraction if recording_url exists
Action items stored in command_centre_items with item_type: follow_up
Each item includes description, assignee, deadline, linked deal/contact
Fires handoff event action_items.complete
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Post-call: actions → follow-up draft + CRM update
userStories.description: As a sales rep, I want follow-up emails drafted and CRM updated automatically after calls, ready for my approval.
acceptanceCriteria: Handoff from action_items.complete triggers draft-followup-email skill
Email draft stored as CC item with drafted_action containing subject/body/recipients
CRM update prepared as separate CC item with hasApproval: true
If no deal exists, creates CC item suggesting deal creation with pre-filled data
All items require approval (initial tier = approve)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Pipeline: deal stage change → risk rescore + next actions
userStories.description: As a sales rep, I want deal risk auto-reassessed and next best actions suggested when a deal moves stages.
acceptanceCriteria: agent-trigger handler for deal_stage_changed triggers risk rescore
Risk result creates/updates CC item with item_type: risk_alert if risk is elevated
Next best actions added as CC items linked to deal_id
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Pipeline: task overdue, proposal sent, cold deal triggers
userStories.description: As a sales rep, I want escalation reminders for overdue tasks, follow-up timers for proposals, and re-engagement for cold deals.
acceptanceCriteria: Task overdue → CC item with urgency: high + Slack DM
Proposal sent → follow-up timer CC item (3 days default)
Cold deal (no activity >14 days, still open) → CC item + stale-deal-revival sequence
Each trigger respects per-org ability enablement
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: CC inbox feed component
userStories.description: As a sales rep, I want to see a feed of AI-generated action items in my Command Center, prioritized by urgency and relevance.
acceptanceCriteria: CommandCenterInbox component queries command_centre_items where status IN (open, ready)
Sorted by priority_score DESC, urgency, created_at DESC
Supabase realtime subscription for live updates tracked via realtimeMonitor
Empty state: Your AI is watching — items will appear as events happen
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser on localhost:5175

userStories.title: CC item action cards with approve/dismiss/snooze
userStories.description: As a sales rep, I want each CC item to have approve/dismiss/snooze actions so I can act quickly.
acceptanceCriteria: Card shows: icon, title, summary, urgency badge, source agent badge, time ago
Approve executes drafted_action, Dismiss sets status=dismissed, Snooze sets due_date
Approve/dismiss triggers autopilot-record-signal
Expanding card shows full context, action preview, linked entity
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser on localhost:5175

userStories.title: CC primary view swap — inbox first, chat second
userStories.description: As a sales rep, I want the Command Center to show my intelligence inbox by default, with copilot chat as a secondary tab.
acceptanceCriteria: CommandCenter restructured with tabs: Inbox (default) and Chat
Inbox tab renders CommandCenterInbox, Chat tab renders existing copilot
Unread/pending count badge on Inbox tab
Tab state persists in Zustand store
SheetContent uses !top-16 !h-[calc(100vh-4rem)] pattern
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser on localhost:5175

userStories.title: Approval counter + auto-execute progress badge
userStories.description: As a sales rep, I want to see how many times I've approved each action type and how close I am to auto-execute.
acceptanceCriteria: CC item card shows badge: Approved X of these · Y more to auto-execute
Progress reads from autopilot_confidence table
When promotion_eligible=true, shows highlighted nudge
Clicking nudge calls autopilot-evaluate
Badge hidden for items where never_promote=true
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser on localhost:5175

userStories.title: Ability card trigger flow visualization
userStories.description: As a sales rep, I want to see a visual flow of how each ability works — what triggers it, what it does, and how it delivers results.
acceptanceCriteria: AbilityTriggerFlow component renders mini vertical timeline/flow diagram
Shows: Trigger → Processing steps → Delivery
Data from abilityRegistry.ts: triggerType, stepCount, eventType, defaultChannels
Different icons for cron/event/chain trigger types
Renders inside AbilityDetailSheet popover
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser on localhost:5175

userStories.title: Ability card live stats panel
userStories.description: As a sales rep, I want to see when an ability last ran, how often it succeeds, and how many times it's fired.
acceptanceCriteria: RPC fetches stats from agent_trigger_runs + agent_schedule_runs per ability
Shows: last run (relative), total runs (30 days), success rate %, average duration
Color-coded: green ≥90%, yellow 70-89%, red <70%
Never run state if no executions found
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser on localhost:5175

userStories.title: Ability card inline threshold editing + channel toggles
userStories.description: As a sales rep, I want to adjust when abilities fire and toggle delivery channels right from the ability card.
acceptanceCriteria: Timing threshold input (number + unit) for time-based abilities
Changes saved to user_sequence_preferences or agent_triggers per user/org
Delivery channel toggles (Slack/Email/In-App) using Switch components
Disabled state for channels requiring unconnected integrations
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser on localhost:5175

userStories.title: Integration health heartbeat edge function
userStories.description: As a sales rep, I want the system to check my integrations every 2 hours and alert me via Slack DM if something breaks.
acceptanceCriteria: New integration-health-heartbeat edge function deployed with --no-verify-jwt
Cron every 2 hours via pg_cron
Checks: Google Calendar sync freshness, Slack token validity, HubSpot status, Fathom expiry
Creates CC item via emitCCItem for failures + sends Slack DM
Deduplicates: max 1 alert per integration per 24h
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Slack delivery audit trail
userStories.description: As an admin, I want every Slack message attempt logged so I can diagnose why didn't I get that notification.
acceptanceCriteria: Migration creates slack_delivery_log table
deliverySlack.ts logs every attempt with success/failure + reason
Index on (user_id, created_at)
Queryable from brain health dashboard
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Circuit breakers for cron jobs
userStories.description: As a sales rep, I want failing heartbeats to auto-disable and alert me via Slack DM instead of silently failing forever.
acceptanceCriteria: Track consecutive failures per cron job
After 5 failures: disable cron, send Slack DM
Auto-retry after 1h cooldown, extend to 4h then 24h on continued failure
Recovery DM sent when auto-retry succeeds
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Event replay trail on CC items
userStories.description: As a sales rep, I want to see a visual trace of how each CC item was created — what triggered it, what ran, where anything failed.
acceptanceCriteria: EventReplayTrail component renders horizontal breadcrumb trail
Each step: icon, label, status (complete/running/failed/skipped)
Data from sequence_executions + agent_trigger_runs via source_event_id
Failed steps show error on hover/click
Show trace link on CC item cards
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser on localhost:5175

userStories.title: Graduated autonomy UI in CC inbox + settings
userStories.description: As a sales rep, I want to see my autonomy level per action type and get in-app promotion proposals.
acceptanceCriteria: Settings section: AI Autonomy showing each action type with tier, confidence, approval count
In CC inbox: promotion banner when pending_promotion_nudge=true with Accept/Not Now
Accept calls promotion engine, Not Now snoozes 7 days
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser on localhost:5175
userStories.notes: Backend 80% built — this is the UI layer

userStories.title: Implement CC auto-execute action dispatch
userStories.description: As a platform engineer, I want CC auto-execute to actually dispatch actions instead of being a stub.
acceptanceCriteria: Replace stub in cc-auto-execute (CC12-007) with real dispatch
Route by drafted_action.type: send_email, update_crm, create_task, send_slack
Rate limits preserved: 10/day total, 3/day external
Pre-exec state stored for undo, signal recorded as auto_executed
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Critical for graduated autonomy to work end-to-end

userStories.title: Learn from edits — capture edited content on approval
userStories.description: As a sales rep, I want my edits to AI-generated drafts captured so the system can learn my preferences.
acceptanceCriteria: Edited content saved to hitl_pending_approvals.edited_content on approval
HITL callback sends edited_content (not original) when edits exist
Signal recorded as approved_edited in autopilot system
Both original and edited preserved for diff analysis
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Learn from edits — diff extraction + preference memories
userStories.description: As a sales rep, I want the system to analyze my edits, extract style preferences, and remember them.
acceptanceCriteria: New extract-edit-feedback edge function triggers on approved HITL with edited_content
Claude compares original vs edited: what changed, inferred preference, confidence
Creates copilot_memories with category: preference
Deduplicates: updates existing preference confidence instead of duplicating
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Inject learned preferences into draft generation
userStories.description: As a sales rep, I want future drafts to reflect my learned writing preferences so I edit less over time.
acceptanceCriteria: Draft generation skills fetch recent preference memories before generating
Preferences injected into system prompt
Contact-specific preferences override general preferences
No regression if no preferences exist yet
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Consolidated morning brain DM
userStories.description: As a sales rep, I want one Slack DM each morning covering everything: auto-executed actions, integration alerts, meetings, deals, overnight activity.
acceptanceCriteria: Merge slack-morning-brief and agent-morning-briefing into single builder
Sections: auto-exec overnight, integration alerts, follow-ups, meetings, deals, overnight activity, priorities
Block Kit with action buttons per section
Timezone-aware per-user timing, deduplicate (1 brief/day)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Two parallel systems exist — consolidate into one

userStories.title: Morning DM reply more thread handler
userStories.description: As a sales rep, I want to reply more to my morning brief and get expanded details in a thread.
acceptanceCriteria: Slack handler detects more reply or reaction in morning brief DM thread
Responds with expanded view per section
Supports section-specific: more meetings, more deals, more overnight
Handler registered in slack-events or slack-interactive router
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Cross-deal pattern intelligence
userStories.description: As a sales rep, I want the AI to spot patterns across my pipeline and proactively suggest what works.
acceptanceCriteria: Wire agent-pipeline-patterns + agent-engagement-patterns outputs as CC items
Insights with item_type: insight, urgency: normal
Include: pattern description, evidence, suggested action, confidence
Max 2 pattern insights per user per week
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Brain health dashboard on Agent Demo page
userStories.description: As an admin, I want to see brain health: cron status, trigger rates, errors, DLQ depth, Slack delivery rates.
acceptanceCriteria: New Brain Health section on Agent Demo page
Shows: cron jobs with last run + status, trigger rate (24h), error rate, DLQ depth, Slack success rate
Color-coded: green (healthy), yellow (warnings), red (failures)
Single RPC aggregating schedule runs, trigger runs, dead letters, delivery log
Auto-refresh every 30 seconds
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser on localhost:5175

userStories.title: First 24h onboarding — brain does something visible
userStories.description: As a new user, I want the brain to do something visible within my first 24 hours so I know it's working.
acceptanceCriteria: First login or first calendar sync triggers agent-initial-scan
Creates 2-5 CC items: stale deals, upcoming meetings to prep, contacts needing follow-up
If calendar connected: prep next meeting immediately
If no data: CC item says Connect your calendar and I'll prep your first meeting
Items styled with Welcome badge
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser on localhost:5175
